threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look bewildered when someone offers you 'butter biscuits' at the local café, instead of just cookies. That's a rookie mistake!
Caught a whiff of jollof rice on the 35 last night. Some bloke was having a full-on feast at the back. It's a vibe you just can't get on the Tube.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was local? Watching the bus driver negotiate with a flock of geese blocking the road on Hermannstraße.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a Späti with a full craft beer fridge and handmade vegan kebabs. It's a late-night local treasure.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they’ve navigated the BART delays and still make it to Tacolicious before the lunch rush. That's real dedication.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the late-night crowd from the back of a 38 Geary. Surprise duet with a busker on the sidewalk. Classic SF vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the locals are at the parks on weekends. Meanwhile, many of us are tucked away in our kitchens, perfecting our Sunday paella.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the new ones by how they stare at the metro map like it's a treasure map—real locals know it's just a game of patience.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'The Night Tube' like it’s some trendy new concept instead of the chaotic, sleep-deprived reality it is.
Heard a guy on the 2 bus chatting about how he saw a fox stealing chips from a kebab shop. Just another night in Brixton, I guess.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a toddler argue with their parent in Turkish about which döner shop was better—classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bar that serves Schnitzel with vegan alternatives, blending tradition and modern tastes in a way that feels uniquely Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to pack an umbrella for the fog that rolls in every evening, but leaves it at home until then.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a musician playing a haunting tune on the 31. Every rider just paused, lost in that moment—guess we all needed a break from the chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's squares are just for pictures, but locals actually use them to unwind after work. It's a different vibe come sunset.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still look for a metro map on the wall instead of pulling it up on their phone.
